XVoiceCreateTwoToOneMixingDecoder

Returns an IXVoiceDecoder interface for a two-to-one mixing decoder.

HRESULT XVoiceCreateTwoToOneMixingDecoder(
    DWORD dwMaxStreams,
    LPXVOICEDECODER *ppMediaObject
);

Parameters

dwMaxStreams
[in] Maximum number of streams supported by the decoder. This parameter should be set to the maximum number of incoming voice streams the game will need to decode.
ppMediaObject
[out] Address of a pointer of type LPXVOICEDECODER. This pointer receives the address of the IXVoiceDecoder interface.

Return Values

TBD

Remarks

The interface is returned with a reference count of one. If only a single thread is accessing the decoder, the title need not call the AddRef method on the interface.

When the title no longer needs the decoder it should call the Release method on the interface. Calling the Release method deallocates the memory associated with the decoder.

The IXVoiceDecoder::ProcessMultiple method for the two-to-one mixing decoder takes two input channels, decompresses them, then mixes the decompressed results into a single output channel.

The XVoice API tracks the history of each individual voice stream. The dwMaxStreams parameter should be set to the maximum number of incoming voice streams the game will need to decode. This will allow histories to be tracked for all incoming voices. When calling the IXVoiceDecoder::ProcessMultiple method, set the pContext member of the input XMEDIAPACKETs to point to the appropriate XVOICE_STREAM_INDEX variables representing the index of the current voice stream being decoded.

Requirements

Header: Declared in Xvoice.h.

Import Library: Use Xvoice.lib.

See Also

Voice Chat Functions | IXVoiceDecoder | XVoiceCreateFourToFourFullDecoder, XVoiceCreateFourToOneMixingDecoder, XVoiceCreateOneToOneDecoder
